interfaces.name: Discipline
interfaces.signature: enum Discipline: String, Codable, CaseIterable, Identifiable { case boulder, route }
interfaces.notes: Single fixed set, drives DisciplineIconToggle and per-discipline load split.

interfaces.name: ClimbingGrade
interfaces.signature: enum ClimbingGrade: Codable, Hashable { case vScale(Int); case yds(String); case french(String) }
interfaces.notes: Single discrete grade only, no slash/range values ever constructed. String cases hold canonical labels like "5.10a" or "6b+" from a fixed lookup list, not free text.

interfaces.name: EffortLevel
interfaces.signature: enum EffortLevel: Int, Codable, CaseIterable, Identifiable { case easy = 1, moderate, hard, veryHard, max; var accessibleLabel: String { get } }
interfaces.notes: Defaults to .moderate in the log sheet so save is never blocked on an unset effort.

interfaces.name: Verdict
interfaces.signature: enum Verdict: String, Codable { case train, easyDay, rest }
interfaces.notes: Rendered word in VerdictCard; never shown without ReasoningLine alongside it.

interfaces.name: PhaseLabel
interfaces.signature: enum PhaseLabel: String, Codable, CaseIterable { case base, build, peak, deload }
interfaces.notes: Free-tier one-line label; must pair with non-color signal per locked accessibility rule.

interfaces.name: DayKey
interfaces.signature: struct DayKey: Hashable, Codable { let stringValue: String; static func make(from date: Date, calendar: Calendar) -> DayKey }
interfaces.notes: Canonical local-calendar day key (yyyy-MM-dd in Calendar.current), used by both LoadMath and DailyLoadSnapshotEntity so no lane invents its own date-string format.

interfaces.name: DisciplineSubEntry
interfaces.signature: struct DisciplineSubEntry: Identifiable, Codable, Hashable { let id: UUID; var discipline: Discipline; var grade: ClimbingGrade; var attemptCount: Int; var didSend: Bool; var effort: EffortLevel }
interfaces.notes: attemptCount defaults to 1 in the sheet, not 0.

interfaces.name: SessionRecord
interfaces.signature: struct SessionRecord: Identifiable, Codable, Hashable { let id: UUID; var timestampUTC: Date; var subEntries: [DisciplineSubEntry]; var isRestDayOverride: Bool }
interfaces.notes: Value-type mirror of SessionEntity; this is what LoadMath and unit tests operate on, never @Model objects directly.

interfaces.name: LoadSnapshot
interfaces.signature: struct LoadSnapshot: Identifiable, Codable, Hashable { var id: DayKey; var blendedAcute: Double; var blendedChronic: Double; var boulderAcute: Double; var boulderChronic: Double; var routeAcute: Double; var routeChronic: Double; var phase: PhaseLabel? }
interfaces.notes: One row per local calendar day; Trends reads these directly, never recomputes from raw sessions at render time.

interfaces.name: TodayCardState
interfaces.signature: enum TodayCardState: Equatable { case initialLoad; case corruptStore; case calibrating(dayCount: Int, rawAcuteLoad: Double); case normal(verdict: Verdict, reasoning: String, acuteLoad: Double, chronicLoad: Double); case gatedRest(reasoning: String, bannerDismissed: Bool) }
interfaces.notes: Single enum driving VerdictCard; computed by LoadMath, cached on TrainingLoadEngine, never derived live inside a view body.

interfaces.name: PhaseState
interfaces.signature: struct PhaseState: Equatable { var label: PhaseLabel?; var basis: String }
interfaces.notes: label is nil pre-14-day-calibration; basis is the one-line trend-direction reasoning shown next to the label.

interfaces.name: LoadFormula
interfaces.signature: enum LoadFormula { static func contribution(for entry: DisciplineSubEntry) -> Double; static func disciplineMultiplier(for discipline: Discipline) -> Double }
interfaces.notes: load = normalizedGrade * attemptCount * disciplineMultiplier; documented as a tunable heuristic, never shown to the user as a grade-equivalence claim.

interfaces.name: GradeScale
interfaces.signature: enum GradeScale { static func normalizedValue(for grade: ClimbingGrade) -> Double }
interfaces.notes: Invisible cross-discipline index; deterministic lookup table, never surfaced in UI as a grade-equivalence.

interfaces.name: LoadMath
interfaces.signature: enum LoadMath { static func computeTodayState(sessions: [SessionRecord], referenceDate: Date, calendar: Calendar, gateAcknowledgedToday: Bool) -> TodayCardState; static func computeSnapshot(sessions: [SessionRecord], dayKey: DayKey, calendar: Calendar) -> LoadSnapshot; static func computePhaseState(snapshots: [LoadSnapshot]) -> PhaseState; static func affectedRecomputeRange(editedDate: Date, today: Date, calendar: Calendar) -> [DayKey] }
interfaces.notes: Pure, stateless, table-driven-testable. affectedRecomputeRange returns editedDate...today capped at 28 days back from today.

interfaces.name: SessionEntity
interfaces.signature: @Model final class SessionEntity { @Attribute(.unique) var id: UUID; var timestampUTC: Date; var isRestDayOverride: Bool; @Relationship(deleteRule: .cascade) var subEntries: [DisciplineSubEntryEntity] }
interfaces.notes: SwiftData source of truth; not excluded from device/iCloud backup.

interfaces.name: DisciplineSubEntryEntity
interfaces.signature: @Model final class DisciplineSubEntryEntity { @Attribute(.unique) var id: UUID; var disciplineRaw: String; var gradeRaw: String; var attemptCount: Int; var didSend: Bool; var effortRaw: Int; var session: SessionEntity? }
interfaces.notes: gradeRaw stores canonical label string, decoded via ClimbingGrade parser.

interfaces.name: DailyLoadSnapshotEntity
interfaces.signature: @Model final class DailyLoadSnapshotEntity { @Attribute(.unique) var dayKeyString: String; var blendedAcute: Double; var blendedChronic: Double; var boulderAcute: Double; var boulderChronic: Double; var routeAcute: Double; var routeChronic: Double; var phaseRaw: String? }
interfaces.notes: Derived cache, rewritten by TrainingLoadEngine.recompute() for the bounded affected range only.

interfaces.name: SessionRepository
interfaces.signature: protocol SessionRepository { func fetchAll() throws -> [SessionRecord]; func save(_ session: SessionRecord) throws; func update(_ session: SessionRecord) throws; func delete(id: UUID) throws; func fetchSnapshots(from: DayKey, through: DayKey) throws -> [LoadSnapshot]; func upsertSnapshots(_ snapshots: [LoadSnapshot]) throws }
interfaces.notes: Seam between LoadMath/TrainingLoadEngine and SwiftData; mockable for tests without ModelContainer.

interfaces.name: SwiftDataSessionRepository
interfaces.signature: final class SwiftDataSessionRepository: SessionRepository { init(modelContext: ModelContext) }
interfaces.notes: Concrete SwiftData-backed implementation.

interfaces.name: ModelContainerBootstrap
interfaces.signature: enum ModelContainerBootstrap { static func makeContainer() -> Result<ModelContainer, Error> }
interfaces.notes: Manual construction, never .modelContainer(for:); failure routes AppRootView to CorruptStoreRecoveryView instead of crashing.

interfaces.name: TrainingLoadEngine
interfaces.signature: @Observable @MainActor final class TrainingLoadEngine { private(set) var todayState: TodayCardState; private(set) var phaseState: PhaseState; private(set) var snapshots: [LoadSnapshot]; init(repository: SessionRepository, calendar: Calendar); func recompute(referenceDate: Date); func saveSession(_ session: SessionRecord) throws; func updateSession(_ session: SessionRecord) throws; func deleteSession(id: UUID) throws }
interfaces.notes: recompute() is a plain synchronous MainActor method, not actor-isolated or async — races are structurally impossible since every trigger already runs on the main actor.

interfaces.name: EntitlementStore
interfaces.signature: @Observable @MainActor final class EntitlementStore { private(set) var isPro: Bool; func setPro(_ value: Bool) }
interfaces.notes: Real toggle shipping in Release; backed by UserDefaults; unrelated to and physically separate from DebugHistorySeeder.

interfaces.name: GateAcknowledgmentStore
interfaces.signature: struct GateAcknowledgmentStore { func hasAcknowledged(day: DayKey) -> Bool; func acknowledge(day: DayKey) }
interfaces.notes: UserDefaults-backed, not SwiftData; covers the ack-without-logging gate-clear path.

interfaces.name: DebugHistorySeeder
interfaces.signature: #if DEBUG enum DebugHistorySeeder { static func seed(weeks: Int, into repository: SessionRepository) throws } #endif
interfaces.notes: Physically absent from Release binary; also backs XCTest fixture generation via the same underlying array-builder used by LoadMathTests.

interfaces.name: CSVExporter
interfaces.signature: enum CSVExporter { static func makeCSV(sessions: [SessionRecord]) -> String }
interfaces.notes: Pro feature; hands off to the standard share sheet, no background upload.

interfaces.name: AppRootView
interfaces.signature: struct AppRootView: View { var body: some View }
interfaces.notes: Switches between TabView and CorruptStoreRecoveryView based on ModelContainerBootstrap result; owns the single shared isLoggingSession sheet-presentation state consumed by all four tabs' toolbar + buttons; always opens on the Today tab, no tab-selection restoration.

interfaces.name: TodayView
interfaces.signature: struct TodayView: View { var body: some View }
interfaces.notes: Renders VerdictCard + GateBanner driven purely by TrainingLoadEngine.todayState.

interfaces.name: VerdictCard
interfaces.signature: struct VerdictCard: View { let state: TodayCardState; var body: some View }
interfaces.notes: Single enum-driven component, opacity+scale ~250ms transitions between cases, no spring/bounce.

interfaces.name: GateBanner
interfaces.signature: struct GateBanner: View { let reasoning: String; let onAcknowledge: () -> Void; var body: some View }
interfaces.notes: Fires UIAccessibility.post(.announcement) on appear without stealing VoiceOver focus.

interfaces.name: LogSessionSheet
interfaces.signature: struct LogSessionSheet: View { var body: some View }
interfaces.notes: Confirm-then-dismiss save; attemptCount defaults to 1, effort defaults to .moderate, grade wheel defaults to last logged grade or a mid-range value on first use.

interfaces.name: HistoryListView
interfaces.signature: struct HistoryListView: View { var body: some View }
interfaces.notes: Empty-state copy distinct from Today's calibrating copy.

interfaces.name: EditBackdateSheet
interfaces.signature: struct EditBackdateSheet: View { let mode: Mode; var body: some View }
interfaces.notes: Shared create/edit-mode variant, not two separate views.

interfaces.name: TrendsView
interfaces.signature: struct TrendsView: View { var body: some View }
interfaces.notes: Load/Phase segmented control; reads LoadSnapshot rows directly, does no load math itself.

interfaces.name: TrendsChartView
interfaces.signature: struct TrendsChartView: View { let snapshots: [LoadSnapshot]; let isPro: Bool; var body: some View }
interfaces.notes: Swift Charts; phase-band background wash carries a text/pattern signal in addition to color.

interfaces.name: ProLockedOverlay
interfaces.signature: struct ProLockedOverlay: View { var body: some View }
interfaces.notes: Reused across Trends discipline-split and retroactive phase timeline teasers.

interfaces.name: ExportShareCardRenderer
interfaces.signature: enum ExportShareCardRenderer { static func renderImage(snapshot: LoadSnapshot, phase: PhaseLabel?) -> UIImage }
interfaces.notes: Uses SwiftUI ImageRenderer over a purpose-built card view, not a raw screenshot.

interfaces.name: SettingsView
interfaces.signature: struct SettingsView: View { var body: some View }
interfaces.notes: Debug entitlement/seeding controls rendered in a visually distinct section, compiled out entirely in Release for the seeder.

interfaces.name: CorruptStoreRecoveryView
interfaces.signature: struct CorruptStoreRecoveryView: View { let onReset: () -> Void; var body: some View }
interfaces.notes: Sole action is a destructive 'reset local data' button that deletes the store file and reconstructs a fresh ModelContainer; copy is explicit about permanent data loss.

interfaces.name: AccessibilityAnnouncer
interfaces.signature: enum AccessibilityAnnouncer { static func announce(_ message: String) }
interfaces.notes: Wraps UIAccessibility.post(notification: .announcement, argument:); used by GateBanner and any future state-change announcements.

interfaces.name: EmptyStateView
interfaces.signature: struct EmptyStateView: View { let title: String; let message: String; let systemImage: String; var body: some View }
interfaces.notes: Shared empty/error-state shell reused by History, Trends, and Settings so copy stays distinct per-screen while layout stays consistent.
